key_insight: PtyRANNOSAUR uses convolutional autoencoders to turn 4D-STEM ptychography data into atomic-resolution phase images in seconds, enabling near-live reconstruction with sub-angstrom accuracy.
engineering_relevance: The method can dramatically accelerate electron ptychography workflows for materials characterization while maintaining reconstruction quality comparable to the best iterative solvers.
method: Trains CNN autoencoders on a large database of crystal structures.
Tailors separate models to experimental conditions such as voltage, convergence angle, defocus, and thickness.
Accounts for partial coherence, multiple scattering, and scan position errors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports testing on both experimental and simulated data, with reconstructions achieving resolutions below 0.5 Å and speeds 10–100× faster than standard methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on having models trained for the relevant experimental regime, so out-of-distribution samples or conditions may still require careful validation.
paper_title: PtyRANNOSAUR: Ptychography with Robust Artificial Neural Networks Optimized for Sub-Angstrom Accuracy and Ultrafast Reconstruction
